rulings.Case Title: IN RE: PACIFIC MERCHANT SHIPPING ASSOCIATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Pmsa'S Notice Of Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, May 9, 2014, Line 3, PETITIONER PACIFIC MERCHANT SHIPPING ASSOCIATION's Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs. Hearing required. The Court orders Respondents Port Agent and Board of Pilot Commissioners to provide the Court, preferably by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org in advance of the hearing, evidence of the amount of hours spent defending this action. If email is not feasible, this information should be provided to the Court in hard copy by 9:15 am on the date of the hearing. The Court is inclined to issue the following ruling on the prevailing party issue: Granted in part and denied in part. Denied as to Respondent Board of Pilot Commissioners (the "Board"). Petitioner Pacific Merchant Shipping Association ("PMSA") has not established that it prevailed against the Board. Granted as to Respondent Port Agent. The Court of Appeal held that the Port Agent is a public official subject to Public Record Act ("PRA") requests. (Board of Pilot Commissioners for the Bays of San Francisco, San Pablo and Suisin et al. v. Superior Ct. (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 577, 591, "We find that the Port Agent must be considered a state officer, at least when performing the official duties provided by statute or Board regulation.") Although the Court of Appeal held that PMSA did not establish that the "Pilot Logs" were public documents, PMSA "succeed[ed] on [a] significant issue in the litigation and achieve[d] some of the benefit sought in the lawsuit." (Garcia v. Governing Board of Bellflower Unified School District (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1058, 1065.) PMSA has presented evidence showing that prior to the litigation, the Port Agent responded to only one of five PRA requests, with the following disclaimer: "[T]his production of information is done without conceding that all of the materials provided are, in fact, subject to the obligations arising out of your request under the Public Records Act." (Ex. E to PMSA's original verified petition, attached as Exhibit A to the Port Agent's opposition brief.) After the litigation, the Port Agent produced voluminous documents in response to PMSA's requests. (Declaration of Michael Jacob, par. 5.) "Cases denying attorney fees to a plaintiff under the act have done so because substantial evidence supports a finding that the litigation did not cause the [agency] to disclose any of the documents ultimately made available¿" (Los Angeles Times v. Alameda Corridor Transportation Authority (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 1381, 1391, citations omitted.) The evidence supports the finding that the Port Agent responded to PMSA's PRA requests once the Court of Appeal declared the Port Agent a public official. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: YIBRAH TESFA VS. ANNE STAUBOLL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, May 9, 2014, Line 5, RESPONDENT ANNE STAUBOLL's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Demurrer overruled on the grounds of res judicata and failure to obtain leave of court. Taking a petition off calendar and declining to rule on it because the relief sought has become moot does not constitute a judgment on the merits. Hollywood Circle, Inc. v. Dept. of Alcoholic Beverage Control (1961) 55 Cal.2d 728, 733. Moreover, Defendant cites no authority and the court finds none supporting a requirement that Plaintiff obtain leave of court prior to filing this action. Demurrer sustained as to the first through sixth causes of action with leave to amend in order for Plaintiff to allege with specificity the acts or omissions of Defendant that give rise to liability for each claim. Demurrer also sustained as to causes of action one through six on the ground that the Complaint fails to allege compliance with the claim presentation requirement. Gov. Code §§ 810 et seq. Government Code section 915(b) is the governing provision, and it requires claims against the state to be presented to an office of the Victim Compensation and Government Claims Board. As to causes of action four through nine, Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend in order for Plaintiff to allege with specificity the acts or omissions of Defendant that deprived Plaintiff of his constitutional rights. Demurrer overruled on the grounds that Defendant is immune from liability, as immunity is an affirmative defense. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RITA O'FLYNN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, May 9, 2014, Line 21, DEFENDANTs RHAPSODY PAINTING AND ENVIRONMENTAL SERVICES, INC., JOSEPH RUIZ's DEMURRER. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend as to the fifth and seventh causes of action and the entire complaint as to Defendant Joseph Ruiz only. Overruled as to the first - fourth, sixth causes of action. Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege the liability of Defendant Joseph Ruiz as an individual. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege the liability of Defendant Joseph Ruiz as an individual. For the first - fourth, and sixth causes of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege each cause of action. These causes of action are not clearly barred by the statute of limitations. For the fifth cause of action, Plaintiff requests leave to amend in opposition. Plaintiff is granted to leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege that he gave notice of the warranty claim to Defendants prior to filing the Complaint. For the seventh cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege waste. Although Plaintiff alleges that Defendants possessed the property, Plaintiff has not alleged that Defendants had a possessory interest in the subject property where Plaintiff had a reversion interest. (See Avalon Pacific-Santa Ana, L.P. v. HD Supply Repair & Remodel, LLC (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 1183, 1213; 12 Witkin, Cal. Summary (10th ed. 2005) Real Prop, § 371, p. 435.) Defendant raises the statute of limitations argument for the first time in reply, which is improper. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege a possessory interest by Defendants. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted that the Complaint, Second Amended Complaint and Second Supplemental Declaratory were filed with this Court, but not for the truth of the matters asserted therein. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: TOTAL OUTDOOR CORP., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. POSTING ACCESS REVENUE, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Disco Responses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, May 09, 2014, Line 1,PLAINTIFF TOTAL OUTDOOR CORP., A DELAWARE CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses Pro Tem Judge Jeffrey Wohl, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Final Approval Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, May 9, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFFs TERESA CORONA, CATALINA TOVAR, ARNOLDO ALVAREZ and LEOCADIO NAA's Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement; Approval Of Attorneys' Fees And Costs; And Approval Of Class Notification Procedure. Hearing required. Plaintiff's counsel shall provide a list of claimants and the projected recovery amounts as to those class members that did not file claims. The Court is concerned that only 40% of claimants returned claim forms. The Court requests more information as to what specific activities that Plaintiff's counsel expended hours. There are a substantial number of timekeepers involved in this case and the Court is concerned that there may be duplicative efforts. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: GUARDSMARK, LLC VS. DERRICK BOWMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re Joint Request For Entry Of [Proposed] Injunctiona Nd Judgment Pursuant Tpo Ccp 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, May 9, 2014, Line 19, PLAINTIFF GUARDSMARK, LLC's Hearing Re Joint Request For Entry Of Injunction and Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 664.6. Plaintiff and Defendants' joint motion for injunction and judgment is granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving party without need for any appearances. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: EXPERT COMMUNICATIONS, INC VS. GAIL TANAKA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Expert Communications, Inc.'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, May 9, 2014, Line 20, Plaintiff Expert Communications, Inc.'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction. Continued to May 27, 2014 pursuant to the request of Defendant Gail Tanaka. (CCP § 527(d)(4).) The continuance date of May 26, 2014 requested by Defendant Tanaka is a court holiday. Defendant Tanaka may file and serve supplemental opposition no later than May 14, 2014 at 4 pm. Any reply to the supplemental opposition may be filed and served no later than May 20, 2014 at 4 pm. Service shall be effectuated by personal delivery, fax or email. Courtesy copies are to be provided to Department 302 on the date of filing. All exhibits must be separated by exhibit tabs. (CRC 3.1110(f).) The Temporary Restraining Order shall remain in effect until the continuance date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving party without need for any appearances. =(302/AJR)
